reasoning: The FIDE rating system updates officially on the 1st of each month. Therefore, the rating listed on Wikipedia on August 10, 2025, is a virtual certainty to be mathematically identical to the rating listed on August 3, 2025 (meaning an increase of >1% is impossible unless vandalism occurs). For the later dates, a 1% increase in Hou Yifan's Elo requires a jump of roughly 26 points. At the 2600+ level, gaining 26 classical Elo points requires a lengthy sequence of overperformances. Hou Yifan is essentially retired from classical chess, currently engaged primarily as a university professor, and only plays occasional rapid/speed chess online (which does not affect standard FIDE Elo ratings). As such, it is exceedingly unlikely she plays enough classical games to increase her rating by 1% within the next six months. Consequently, my probabilities start at the minimum allowed bound (0.02) and marginally climb to reflect baseline uncertainty over the longer horizon.
